entries.reasoning: The report provides scenario-based reasoning with specific loss figures and affected counts, but none of its named target protocols match the ground-truth top-5% systemic risk set (1015, 1036, etc.), indicating the analysis failed to identify the actual high-risk nodes despite appearing detailed.

The report cites specific scenario metrics and provides actionable supervisory recommendations, but its named targets (USDC, USDT, 118, 111, WETH) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% protocols by weight loss (1015, 1307, 144, etc.), indicating poor alignment with the actual systemic risk set.

The report cites specific metrics (USDC $1.554B, 22.93% loss, protocol 118 $306M de-peg loss) and identifies protocol 118 which appears in the ground truth top-5% set. While most named targets aren't in the ground truth sample shown, the analysis is data-grounded, actionable, and correctly flags concentration and contagion risks.

The report cites specific exposure metrics and scenario projections, and correctly identifies protocols 118 and 111 which appear in the ground-truth top-5% set, though most named targets (WETH, 182, 937, etc.) are not confirmed in the ground truth and it misses the broader systemic set.

The report cites specific exposure metrics and scenario losses, but its named targets (240, 118, 1599, USDC, WBTC, etc.) do not overlap with any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic-risk protocols, indicating poor predictive accuracy despite a coherent narrative.

The report cites specific metrics (WETH $5.62B exposure, 18.58% system loss, scenario distress counts) and correctly contextualizes the November 2022 FTX/CEX stress period, with actionable scenario-based reasoning. However, only protocol 111 from its target list overlaps with the ground-truth top-5% set, so its specific protocol identification is largely unvalidated against the actual weight-loss ranking.

The report cites specific metrics (exposure weights, loss percentages, affected protocol counts) and correctly identifies protocol 111 as a key lending risk, which appears in the ground truth top-5% set. However, most of its named targets (2269, 2786, BUSD, WBTC, etc.) don't overlap with the ground truth systemic-loss list, and the elevated/contagion framing is reasonable given the Nov 2022 FTX context.

The report cites specific exposure metrics and loss scenarios, correctly identifies CEX concentration risk in the FTX-collapse context, and flags protocol 118 which appears in the ground-truth top-5% set; however, most of its named targets (2269, BNB, BUSD, 212, 2, 359) do not match the ground-truth high-weight-loss protocols, limiting precision.

The report correctly identifies protocol 118 as a systemic risk (it appears in the ground truth top-5% set) and provides specific metrics and scenario analysis, but only 1 of its 8 named targets matches the ground truth, and several flagged protocols (2269, 212, BNB) are not in the systemic loss set.

The report cites specific scenario metrics (loss percentages, distress counts) and provides plausible risk narrative grounded in the post-FTX context, but it only correctly identifies one ground-truth protocol (118) among its named targets, missing most of the actual systemic-risk set.

The report cites specific scenario metrics (57.26% network loss, $4.06B contagion, 155 protocols distressed) and identifies plausible systemic risk concentration, but its named targets (CEX 2269, BUSD, USDT, bridges) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% weight-loss protocols, indicating poor predictive alignment despite reasonable narrative grounding.

The report cites specific metrics and scenario analyses with confident detail, but none of its named targets (2269, BUSD, BNB, USDT, etc.) match any protocol in the ground-truth top-5% weight-loss set, indicating the identified systemic risks largely miss the actual high-loss protocols.

The report cites specific exposure weights and a coherent contagion narrative, but its named targets (2269, BUSD, USDC, etc.) do not match any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic protocols, indicating the identified risk factors are largely off-target despite plausible reasoning.

The report cites specific stress scenario metrics and identifies two correct top-5% protocols (118, 111) among its targets, with actionable supervisory recommendations; however it only overlaps on a couple of the ground-truth systemic protocols and emphasizes others (e.g., 2269) not in the loss-ranked set.

The report cites specific exposure figures and scenario losses, but its named targets (CEX 2269, WETH, WBTC, BNB, bridges) do not overlap with the ground-truth top-5% systemic-risk set (numeric protocol IDs like 1061, 111, 113-116), suggesting weak alignment with the actual high-weight-loss nodes despite plausible-sounding analysis.

The report cites specific exposure figures and scenario losses, giving it a structured, data-grounded appearance, but none of its named targets (2269, 2786, 359, USDT, USDC, etc.) match any protocol in the ground-truth top-5% weight-loss set, indicating it misses the actual systemic risk drivers.

The report provides specific stress-scenario metrics and a coherent rationale, but only correctly identifies one protocol (114) from the ground-truth top-5% systemic risk set, with most named targets not matching the actual high-weight-loss protocols.

The report cites specific scenario metrics and provides a structured rationale, but its identified target protocols (937, 2269, 2, WBTC, 118, etc.) do not overlap with any of the ground-truth top-5% systemic risk protocols, indicating it misses the actual highest weight-loss protocols.
